title: quercus lanuginosa var. virgiliana
intro: Quercus lanuginosa var. virgiliana, also known as the Korean oak, is a variety of deciduous tree native to East Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which contribute to its health benefits such as reducing oxidative stress and improving cardiovascular health. The bark and leaves of the Korean oak can be used to prepare herbal remedies, including teas, decoctions, and infusions, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. In herbal preparations, the Korean oak is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties and to create synergistic effects.
